commit.message:
fix: remove ZEN2/ZEN3/SKYLAKE from OpenBLAS DYNAMIC_LIST

OpenBLAS 0.3.31 compiles generic C dot/nrm2 kernels for ZEN2, ZEN3 and
SKYLAKE using AVX2+FMA intrinsics but omits -mfma from the compiler
invocation when DYNAMIC_LIST is set, causing an always_inline target
mismatch build error on all platforms.

Fix: remove the three offending targets:
- Generic list: NEHALEM SANDYBRIDGE HASWELL SKYLAKEX ZEN (was +ZEN2 ZEN3)
- AVX2 list:    HASWELL SKYLAKEX (was +SKYLAKE ZEN2 ZEN3)

ZEN2/ZEN3 users fall back to ZEN kernel (still AVX2); Skylake users fall
back to HASWELL — negligible performance difference in practice.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: cbcbox

commit.message:
fix: rename Alumni.svg to alumni.svg and deploy testing on merge (#93)

## Problem

Two issues were causing the mobile app to misbehave on the testing
environment:

### 1. Missing `alumni.svg` (case sensitivity)
The asset file was `assets/images/Alumni.svg` (capital A) on macOS
(case-insensitive filesystem), but the Flutter-generated code and nginx
on the Linux Docker container expected `assets/images/alumni.svg`
(lowercase). This caused a 404 on every page that renders the alumni
logo.

### 2. Testing environment not updated on PR merge
The `deploy-testing` job only ran on pull request events, not on push to
`main`. So when PR #91 merged, only **production** was updated — the
testing environment kept running the old code.

## Changes
- `assets/images/Alumni.svg` → `assets/images/alumni.svg` (rename, Linux
case fix)
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — `deploy-testing` now also runs on
`push` to `main`

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: ensure TOC section fully hidden on Pages

Move hr separators inside the readme-toc div so they hide too.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add robots.txt to disallow crawlers (#92)

## Summary

Adds a `robots.txt` file to the Flutter web app to disallow all
crawlers.

The IU Alumni platform is a private app and should not be indexed by
search engines. Bots (including OpenAI SearchBot) were hitting
`/robots.txt` and receiving 404 errors — this adds the missing file.

## Changes
- `web/robots.txt` — `Disallow: /` for all user-agents

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: string interning, Object.is, flat(depth), Number() conversion (#443)

* perf: add string interning for property names

Add thread-local string intern pool (HashSet<Rc<str>>) that deduplicates
property name strings across the engine. All property lookups via
get_string_constant() now go through the intern pool, ensuring shared
allocations and enabling O(1) pointer comparison for string equality.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add Object.is and recursive Array.prototype.flat(depth)

- Object.is(x, y) uses SameValue algorithm (+0 !== -0, NaN === NaN)
- Array.prototype.flat(depth) now supports arbitrary depth parameter

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add PlainObject toString, Number() callable conversion

- Add toString handler in proto_lookup for PlainObject returning
  '[object Object]' (Object.prototype.toString default behavior)
- Add __call__ to Number constructor so Number(value) performs
  type conversion (e.g. Number('42') → 42)
- String() and Boolean() already had callable conversion support
- Error.prototype.stack was already implemented

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: scan-build findings — MallocOverflow cast and dead store

IccHeuristicsDataValidation.cpp:447 — cast scanLimit to size_t before
multiplication to avoid alpha.security.MallocOverflow (scanLimit is capped
to 4096 so no actual overflow, but the cast makes intent explicit).

IccHeuristicsRawPost.cpp:1106 — hasSvcn was assigned but never read.
Added spectral tag consistency check: svcn without sdin is suspicious.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add edge midpoint handles for free-form (non-proportional) resize

Four new handles at edge midpoints (top, bottom, left, right) allow
stretching elements along a single axis. Visually distinct from
corner handles (10×4 bars vs 7×7 squares). Corner handles retain
proportional resize behavior.

Edge handle resize math: N/S handles scale only Y axis, E/W handles
scale only X axis, using element viewBox dimensions for normalization.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: move Supabase client init out of component body to prevent SSR errors

createClient() was being called at component initialization level in both
ProtectedRoute and AdminLayout, causing Supabase to throw during Next.js
static prerendering when env vars are not available at build time.

- Remove unused supabase instance from ProtectedRoute (auth uses fetch API)
- Move createClient() inside handleLogout() in AdminLayout so it only
  runs client-side when the user clicks logout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix publish failure: dynamic versioning to prevent 409 Conflict (#16)

* Initial plan

* Fix build and publish failure: use dynamic version to avoid 409 Conflict

The publish workflow failed because version 1.0.0 was already published
to GitHub Packages and GitHub Packages does not allow overwriting existing
versions. This fix makes the version dynamic by reading SDK_VERSION from
the environment (set to 1.0.$GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER in CI), so each publish
gets a unique version number.

Co-authored-by: ahmedaliahmed775 <196491727+ahmedaliahmed775@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix tests creating stray test.md: use [scratch] pseudo-path

Tests that don't need disk I/O were using bare 'test.md' as
the path, causing atomic_write to create a file in the crate
directory. Changed all bare 'test.md' paths to '[scratch]'
which is skipped by the save path.

Tests that actually test disk writes use TempDir-based paths
(unchanged).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

for SHA-256 hashing (ComputeSHA256). The static release build was missing
-lssl -lcrypto, causing undefined symbol errors for EVP_MD_CTX_new,
EVP_sha256, EVP_DigestInit_ex, etc.

commit.message:
Remove redundant abortUpload mutation

The tus-server gem already provides DELETE /files/:uid for aborting uploads.
The abortUpload GraphQL mutation was just a thin wrapper with no added
business logic, so it's redundant.

Keep only:
- finalizeUpload: Essential GraphQL mutation that moves files from
  tmp/tus_uploads to the final destination (Config::Video.settings_upload_path)

Clients now use:
- DELETE /files/:uid (tus REST API) to abort uploads
- finalizeUpload(uploadId) (GraphQL) to promote to production

All tests pass: 34 runs, 65 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix toCurrency persistence: don't overwrite AppStorage on every load

loadConverter() was unconditionally setting toCurrency = list[1] on
every app launch, clobbering the AppStorage-saved value. Now both
fromCurrency and toCurrency are only reset when the saved code is
no longer present in the loaded currency list (i.e. stale/invalid).

commit.message:
Replace custom chunked upload system with tus.io protocol

Implements RFC-like tus resumable upload protocol for large file transfers.
Benefits over custom system:
- Standard protocol with 6 official client libraries (JS, Python, Java, Go, Android, Swift)
- Resumable uploads after network failures
- Built-in checksum validation
- Automatic expiration of incomplete uploads (48 hours)
- Works like FTP: chunk-based, atomic, multipart
- Proven at Vimeo and Google scale

Changes:
- Add tus-server gem (~> 2.0)
- Mount tus-server at /files endpoint (REST API)
- Rewrite FinalizeUpload & AbortUpload mutations for tus
- Remove custom InitiateUpload mutation (tus handles via POST /files)
- Remove custom API controller (tus handles chunks via PATCH /files/:uid)
- Remove AssemblyJob (tus handles assembly natively)

API changes:
- Client initiates: POST /files → tus server returns Location header with UID
- Client resumes: HEAD /files/:uid → tus server returns Upload-Offset
- Client sends chunk: PATCH /files/:uid with Content-Range header
- Client finalizes: GraphQL finalizeUpload(uploadId) mutation
- Client aborts: DELETE /files/:uid or GraphQL abortUpload mutation

All tests pass: 36 runs, 72 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Reimplement Agenda as a BQL page

SPC a a now opens a page titled 'Agenda' instead of a custom
overlay. If no Agenda page exists, one is created from a built-in
template with three BQL query blocks:
- Overdue: tasks | where not done and due < today
- This Week: tasks | where not done and due this week
- No Due Date: tasks | where not done and due = none

The page is a regular Bloom page — editable, searchable via
SPC f f, customisable. Users can add sections, change queries,
mix with prose. The old agenda overlay code (AgendaState,
handle_agenda_key) remains for now but is no longer the primary
agenda UI.

commit.message:
Fix POSIX-only path splitting in file watcher glob base-path extraction (#675)

`extractBaseDir` used hardcoded `'/'` for splitting/joining glob paths,
causing it to fall through to the `'.'` fallback on Windows and silently
breaking file watchers.

### Changes

- **`file-watch-service.ts`**: Normalize backslashes to forward slashes
via `glob.replace(/\\/g, '/')` before splitting, so Windows paths are
handled consistently on all platforms. Export `extractBaseDir` for
testability. Add `console.warn` when the function silently falls back to
`'.'` for globs that don't start with a wildcard, surfacing potential
misconfiguration instead of silently watching the wrong directory.
- **`file-watch-service.test.ts`**: Add unit tests for `extractBaseDir`
covering POSIX globs, Windows backslash paths, mixed separators, all
wildcard types, empty string and root-only edge cases, and warning
behavior on fallback.

```typescript
// Before
const parts = glob.split('/');
// ...
const base = baseParts.join('/');

// After
const normalized = glob.replace(/\\/g, '/');
const parts = normalized.split('/');
// ...
const base = baseParts.join('/');
if (!base && !glob.startsWith('*') && ...) {
  console.warn(`extractBaseDir: Could not extract base from glob "${glob}", falling back to '.'`);
}
return base || '.';
```

**Note:** PR #661 (merged to main) independently fixed the same core bug
using the `replace(/\\/g, '/')` approach. This PR resolves merge
conflicts with that change, adopts the same normalization strategy, and
adds the warning guard and comprehensive `extractBaseDir` unit tests on
top.

Windows, watches wrong directory (`.` fallback) or silently misses
files.
> 
> ## Impact
> File watching is broken on Windows -- plugins relying on file watchers
will not receive expected events.
> 
> ## Fix Suggestion
> Use `path.sep` or `path.parse`/`path.join` for platform-independent
path handling, or normalize globs to POSIX format before
processing.</issue_description>

commit.message:
Phase 9.4: Render Integration Bridge

Implement the render integration bridge that connects the widget tree
to the rendering pipeline, completing the frame lifecycle management.

## Architecture

The RenderBridge manages the complete frame lifecycle:
1. Widget tree traversal via DisplayListEmitter interface
2. DisplayList emission with damage tracking
3. Backend submission (GPU or software)
4. Present to compositor

## Implementation

- render.go: Core RenderBridge type with damage tracking (~188 LOC)
  * MarkDirty() / MarkRegionDirty() for damage tracking
  * Render() handles full redraw vs. regional updates
  * walkWidget() recursively collects DisplayList commands
  * Present() exports frames to compositor
  * Thread-safe with mutex protection

- app.go: Integration into App and Window (~66 LOC)
  * RenderBridge added to Window struct
  * RenderFrame(), Redraw(), RedrawRegion() public API
  * Automatic rendering in eventLoop via renderFrames()
  * Window resize events trigger automatic redraws
  * SetRootWidget() marks bridge dirty on changes

- render_test.go: Comprehensive test coverage (~402 LOC)
  * 16 tests covering all RenderBridge functionality
  * Mock renderer and widget implementations
  * Tests for damage tracking, widget tree walking, thread safety
  * All tests passing (100% pass rate)

## Quality Metrics

- Zero critical regressions
- All new functions ≤10 cyclomatic complexity
- walkWidget: 8.8 complexity (below threshold)
- Render: 9.6 complexity (below threshold)
- Total new code: 677 LOC (197 implementation + 402 tests + 78 integration)

## Milestone Achieved

Applications using the public API can now render frames through the
complete backend infrastructure with automatic damage tracking and
optimal redraw scheduling.

Completes ROADMAP.md Phase 9.4.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add 'dashboard' to SPA catch-all exclusion list

The SPA catch-all /{path:path} was serving index.html for /dashboard/*
API paths, preventing the dashboard router from handling the request.
This caused /dashboard/health (and potentially /dashboard/all) to return
the React SPA instead of JSON API responses when accessed directly.

commit.message:
Bypass HttpContext in hot path: direct Kestrel feature access (#1298, #1304)

Refactor BmwContext to resolve Kestrel features once and store them
directly — no DefaultHttpContext allocation in the request pipeline.

Pipeline changes:
- BmwApplication.ProcessRequestAsync passes BmwContext directly to
  RequestHandler (eliminates double context creation)
- RequestHandler takes BmwContext instead of HttpContext
- All pipeline helpers (ApplySecurityHeaders, ApplyCors, IsHttpsRequest,
  BuildHttpsRedirectUrl, IsAjaxRequest, TryGetForwarded*) operate on
  BmwContext using direct feature accessors
- BenchmarkEndpoints.TryHandle takes BmwContext
- TenantRegistry.ResolveForRequest takes BmwContext

BmwContext changes:
- Stores IHttpRequestFeature, IHttpResponseFeature, IHttpResponseBodyFeature,
  IHttpConnectionFeature, IFeatureCollection directly
- Direct response accessors: StatusCode, ResponseHeaders, ContentType,
  ContentLength, HasResponseStarted, WriteResponseAsync, ClearResponse
- Direct request accessors: RequestHeaders, RequestScheme, IsHttps
- HttpContext is now lazy — only allocated when handler code needs the
  full ASP.NET API surface (cookies, forms, DI)
- PageMetaData/PageContext stored directly on BmwContext instead of
  HttpContext.Items — eliminates Items dictionary lookups
- CspNonce stored directly — no HttpContext.Items roundtrip

HtmlRenderer/IHtmlRenderer:
- RenderPage takes BmwContext instead of HttpContext
- Reads PageInfo, App, CspNonce from BmwContext fields
- Writes response via BmwContext.ResponseBody (PipeWriter)

Extension methods:
- BmwContext overloads (SetStringValue, SetLoop, AddTable, etc.) now
  operate on BmwContext.PageContext directly instead of delegating
  through HttpContext.Items
- GetCspNonce uses BmwContext.CspNonce field

ApiErrorWriter:
- New BmwContext overload writes JSON directly to PipeWriter
  (IBufferWriter<byte>) — zero intermediate allocation
- Shared WriteErrorBody helper eliminates code duplication

Also: tune socket backlog to 8192 for burst traffic (#1304)

Fixes #1298
Fixes #1304

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

> ## Report Source
> Latent Bugs - 04_LATENT_BUGS.md
> 
> ## Files Affected
> - `src/main/services/agent-system.ts`
> 
> ## Found By
> Claude
> 
> ## Description
> The stale sweep and auto-exit timer call `killAgent` without awaiting
or catching. If `killAgent` rejects (process already dead), the
rejection is unhandled.
> 
> ## Impact
> Unhandled promise rejections can crash the main process in strict
environments or cause silent failures in agent cleanup.
> 
> ## Fix Suggestion
> Await `killAgent` calls and wrap in try/catch, or attach `.catch()`
handlers to suppress expected rejections from already-dead
processes.</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes Agent-Clubhouse/Clubhouse#570

commit.message:
Update WORKFLOWS/make-trailer-pipeline.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Execute BQL queries inline during page render

When the editor renders a page containing ```bql code blocks,
the query is parsed, compiled, and executed against the index.
Results replace the raw query text as RenderedLines:

- Successful queries: result rows with column separators + count footer
- Count queries: single number
- Group counts: key:count pairs
- Errors: warning message in BrokenLink style
- No results: '(no results)' placeholder

The opening fence renders dimmed (SyntaxNoise). Query text is
hidden (not rendered). Closing fence triggers execution.

New infrastructure:
- QueryResultBlock + QueryResultRow in PaneFrame (for future cursor interaction)
- Index::connection() accessor for BQL executor
- execute_bql_for_render() helper in editor/render.rs

All 398 tests pass. BQL queries are now live in any page.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Agent: Test Duration (#7624)

* Agent: Test Duration

* Initial plan

* perf(test): reduce slow performance-regression batch sizes and trim watch-auto-inject delays

- Shrink buildLoopInvariantStressBatchSource(120, 30) → (60, 15) in test 6:
  saves ~500ms while still exercising the same algorithmic guarantee
- Shrink buildLoopInvariantStressBatchSource(320, 60) → (160, 30) in test 7:
  saves ~2.4s (the dominant bottleneck: 3.26s → 0.82s, 4× faster)
- Tighten time thresholds proportionally; any O(n²) regression would still
  produce runtimes thousands of times over the new caps
- Replace setTimeoutPromise(500) → setTimeoutPromise(200) in three
  watch-auto-inject tests; the injection is file-local I/O, completes <50ms

Before: performance-regression.test 5.5s, watch-auto-inject 1.8s
After:  performance-regression.test 2.6s, watch-auto-inject 0.96s
Full suite: 50.6s → 48.4s (–2.2s / –4.3%); lint suite 10s → 7s (–30%)

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Lightpanda CDP adapter with Target-scoped sessions

- Rewrote lightpanda.rs to use Target.createTarget + Target.attachToTarget
  with session-scoped CDP commands (matching Lightpanda's actual protocol)
- Fixed session start to use HTTP validation instead of holding a WS
  connection (Lightpanda only supports one WS connection at a time)
- Fixed module paths in nav.rs and eval.rs to use crate::commands::session
- Each CLI command (navigate, eval) opens a short-lived CDP connection
- Tested live: navigate 81ms, eval works, session info shows engine

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
